back_translation: A man shall not take the wife of his father, and he shall not uncover the corner of his father.
One wounded crushed or with cut genitals shall not enter the assembly of the Lord.
One of illegitimate birth shall not enter the assembly of the Lord; even the tenth generation shall not enter for him the assembly of the Lord.
An Ammonite and a Moabite shall not enter the assembly of the Lord; even the tenth generation shall not enter for them the assembly of the Lord forever,

because of the matter that they did not meet you with bread and with water on the way when you came out of Egypt, and that he hired against you Balaam son of Beor from Pethor of Aram-naharaim to curse you.
But the Lord your God was not willing to listen to Balaam, and the Lord your God turned for you the curse into a blessing, because the Lord your God loves you.
You shall not seek their peace and their good, all your days, forever.
You shall not detest an Edomite, for he is your brother. You shall not detest an Egyptian, for a foreigner you were in his land.
Sons who shall be born to them, the third generation, shall enter for them the assembly of the Lord.
When you go out as a camp against your enemies, then you shall keep yourself from every evil thing.
When there is among you a man who is not clean from a nocturnal emission, then he shall go out to outside the camp; he shall not come into the midst of the camp.
And it shall be, when evening turns, he shall wash in water, and when the sun comes in, he shall come to the midst of the camp.
And a place shall be for you outside the camp, and you shall go out there outside.
And a peg shall be for you on your equipment, and it shall be, when you sit outside, you shall dig in it, and you shall turn and cover your excrement.

For the Lord your God walks in the midst of your camp, to save you and to give your enemies before you; and your camp shall be holy, and He shall not see in you a shameful thing and turn away from after you.
You shall not deliver a slave to his master, who escapes to you from with his master.
With you he shall dwell, in your midst, in the place that he chooses, in one of your gates, in what is good for him; you shall not oppress him.
There shall not be a shrine prostitute from the daughters of Israel, and there shall not be a male shrine prostitute from the sons of Israel.
You shall not bring the wages of a prostitute and the price of a dog into the house of the Lord your God for any vow, for an abomination of the Lord your God are both of them.
You shall not lend on interest to your brother, interest of money, interest of food, interest of any thing that is lent on interest.

To the foreigner you may lend on interest, but to your brother you shall not lend on interest, so that the Lord your God may bless you in everything that your hand sends forth, upon the land that you are entering there to possess.
When you make a vow to the Lord your God, you shall not delay to pay it, for surely the Lord your God will require it from you, and it shall be in you sin.
But when you refrain from vowing, it shall not be in you sin.
What goes out of your lips you shall keep and do, as you have vowed to the Lord your God a freewill offering, what you have spoken with your mouth.
When you enter into the vineyard of your neighbor, then you may eat grapes according to the desire of your soul, your fill, but into your basket you shall not put.
When you enter into the standing grain of your neighbor, then you may pluck ears with your hand, but a sickle you shall not wave over the standing grain of your neighbor.
